title: Song. Metempsychosis.
content:
When Grief comes this way by
    With her wan lip and drooping eye,
    Bid her welcome, woo her boldly;
    Soon she'll look on thee less coldly.

    Her tears soon cease to flow.
    'Tis now not Grief but Joy we know;
    From her smiling face the roses
    Tell the glad metempsychosis.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Thomas Runciman, titled "Song. Metempsychosis.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
